title: ajuga vulgaris subsp. fallax
intro: Ajuga vulgaris subsp. fallax, also known as bugleweed, is a flowering plant species in the mint family native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which may help to treat various health conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and hyperthyroidism. The health benefits of bugleweed include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, regulating thyroid function, and supporting immune system health. Bugleweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
